description: Fiddle Leaf Fig (Ficus lyrata) is a trendy houseplant toxic to cats. As a member of the Ficus genus, it produces a milky latex sap containing ficin, a proteolytic enzyme, along with irritant compounds that cause gastrointestinal upset when swallowed and skin irritation on contact. The large, dramatic leaves may attract curious cats.
name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: ficin (proteolytic enzyme) and irritant compounds in the latex sap
Mechanism: ficin irritates the mucosal lining of the mouth, oesophagus, and stomach; sap contact causes localised dermatitis
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, decreased appetite; contact with sap may cause skin redness or itching
Insoluble calcium oxalates may also contribute to oral irritation in some Ficus species
Symptoms are typically mild; however, repeated nibbling can lead to ongoing GI irritation — relocate the plant away from cats

symptoms.name: Oral Irritation (Pain and burning sensation in the mouth, tongue, and lips)
symptoms.onset: Immediate (Upon chewing or biting into the leaf/stem).

symptoms.name: Drooling / Hypersalivation
symptoms.onset: Immediate to Acute (Shortly after oral contact).
